summary:
Kell alloimmunization is strongly linked to severe hemolytic disease of the fetus and newborn due to antibody-mediated destruction of red blood cells and suppression of erythropoiesis. It has no known association with fetal thrombocytopenia or neonatal alloimmune thrombopetopenia, as there is no documented mechanism or epidemiological link connecting anti-Kell antibodies to platelet damage or reduced platelet counts.  

$\boxed{\text{B}}$ The statement "Kell alloimmunization in pregnancy is associated with fetal thrombocytopenia" is false based on available medical evidence. 

Note: Final summary must contain only two sentences focused strictly on clinically significant facts related directly to the query about fetal thrombocytopenia.
